pass1_value.note: Docs describe consumer key & consumer secret (API keys), HTTP Basic Auth (using consumer key as username and secret as password), and OAuth 1.0a for legacy integrations. JWT/bearer tokens are not listed as built-in methods.

final_value.note: Official WooCommerce REST API docs state WooCommerce uses API keys (Consumer Key/Consumer Secret). Over HTTPS it supports HTTP Basic Authentication using the consumer key as username and consumer secret as password; without SSL it supports OAuth 1.0a.

pass1_value.note: API keys (consumer key & secret) are generated in the WordPress admin (WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API) by site users—no vendor approval required.

final_value.note: Official docs describe creating REST API keys in the WordPress admin under WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API by selecting Add key for a user, generating a consumer key and consumer secret (no vendor approval flow indicated).

pass1_value.note: Public REST docs and self-serve API key creation exist; a developer with access to a WooCommerce site can obtain credentials and use the REST or Store APIs immediately.

final_value.note: WooCommerce's official REST API documentation describes supported authentication (API keys with Basic Auth over HTTPS or OAuth 1.0a) and self-serve REST API key generation in the WooCommerce admin, enabling immediate development for anyone with access to a WooCommerce site.
